critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: The preprocessing and cross-validation design produced unbiased performance estimates for AutoML pipeline selection.
Why wrong: The sequence 'assembled full modeling table → applied target encoding and imputation → created CV folds' is a pipeline leakage design. Cross-validation is valid only when every data transformation that uses outcome information (including target encoding) is re-computed within each fold on training data only. The AutoML search selected its best pipeline based on leaky CV estimates, and the final model was then evaluated on a held-out test set — but if the selected pipeline was inflated by leaky CV, the selected architecture itself is biased toward the training distribution.
Evidence: Re-run AutoML search with fold-specific target encoding. Compare whether the same pipeline architecture wins under a leakage-free search.

Issue 2
Claim: The held-out test set provides an independent evaluation of the selected model.
Why wrong: The test set was used to choose the operating threshold. Using the test set for threshold selection, even for an operationally-constrained target, makes the test set partially a tuning set. The precision at the selected threshold is in-sample optimistic.
Evidence: Reserve the test set for evaluation only. Select threshold on a validation set or through internal cross-validation of the threshold criterion.

Issue 3
Claim: The logistic baseline provides a fair point of comparison.
Why wrong: The baseline was evaluated with its original threshold from a prior project with a different preprocessing pipeline. It has not been re-evaluated under the same preprocessing and threshold protocol as the AutoML system. An apples-to-apples comparison requires both systems to be evaluated under the same pipeline and threshold selection methodology.
Evidence: Re-run logistic regression baseline inside the same fold-specific-target-encoding pipeline with threshold selected from the same validation split.
defender_raw: The AutoML system was designed to find the best pipeline including feature engineering. Separating the feature engineering from the pipeline search is artificial — the target encoding is part of what makes the pipeline valuable. The baseline was not re-engineered because the team considered the improved feature engineering to be part of the AutoML system's advantage, not a separate variable. The threshold choice is operationally constrained to 80% recall, which is not a free optimization parameter.
adjudication_raw: The defender's argument that feature engineering is 'part of the pipeline advantage' conflates the question of whether target encoding is useful with whether it was applied correctly in the evaluation design. The target encoding is valid; applying it before fold creation is the error. On the threshold: the defender repeats that 80% recall is fixed operationally, which is true, but the mechanism of contamination (finding which threshold achieves it on test data) remains. On Issue 3: the defender explicitly argues the baseline should not be re-evaluated — but this directly results in an asymmetric comparison. All three issues stand. Critique wins.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: AutoML pipeline selected using leaky CV estimates — architecture bias possible
Issue 2: test set used for threshold selection — precision estimate in-sample optimistic
Issue 3: baseline not re-evaluated under AutoML protocol — asymmetric comparison

empirical_test.condition: Re-run AutoML search and baseline with fold-specific target encoding; select thresholds from validation data for both; evaluate both on held-out test set under identical conditions
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: AutoML lift at 80% recall is below 1 point or the selected AutoML architecture differs from the leaky search result (indicating pipeline selection was inflated)
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Same pipeline wins under leakage-free search, AutoML still shows 0.02+ ROC-AUC and 3+ point precision gain
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: ROC-AUC gain preserved but different pipeline architecture selected and threshold lift does not replicate
